comments.author: Alexander Terp
comments.body:
Review surfaced two more facets to settle here:

1. Abort with assignment targets is inconsistent with decline. For
   `code, out = confirm $`...`` :
   - Decline ("n") -> code=1, out="" (exit-code semantics).
   - Abort (Ctrl-C) -> code=<error object>, out=null (expression-failure
     semantics, since the abort RadPanic carries no ShellResult).
   So `code` is an int on decline but an error object on abort. Decide whether
   abort should present exit-code-style results (code as int) or keep the error
   object so a catch block can introspect why it bailed.

2. pick()/multipick() raise their abort errors via NewErrorStrf without a code,
   so they surface as RAD20000 (generic runtime), not RAD20010 like
   confirm()/input()/shell-confirm. Align interactive-prompt abort codes as part
   of the holistic decision.

creator: Alexander Terp
description:
Aborting the confirmation prompt for a shell command (`confirm $`...`` or
`--confirm-shell`) with Ctrl-C/Esc currently raises a *catchable* RAD20010
user-input error, chosen for consistency with confirm()/pick()/input().

Sharp edge: a `catch:` block on the command swallows the Ctrl-C and lets the
script continue - arguably surprising for what is fundamentally a safety gate.
Ctrl-C usually means "get me out", not "handle this and keep going".

Open questions to decide as a language concept:
- Should Ctrl-C/Esc at a confirm gate be a hard, non-catchable stop instead?
- Should declining ("n") stay catchable (exit 1 -> "Command exited with code 1"),
  or get a cleaner non-error "skipped" outcome instead of surfacing as an error?
- What should interactive-prompt aborts do *consistently* across the language
  (confirm/pick/input/multipick and shell confirm)? Is a dedicated "user aborted"
  error code, or a SIGINT-style exit code (130), warranted?
- Rad installs no SIGINT handler today; huh intercepts Ctrl-C and returns an
  error. Worth deciding the holistic story.

Context: prompted by a RAD20042 "internal bug" crash when aborting, now fixed -
abort routes through a catchable RAD20010 rather than panicking. Relevant code:
confirmation handling in core/shell.go (executeShellCmd).
title: Rethink shell-confirmation abort (Ctrl-C/Esc) behavior
